title: metadata
description: Common metadata envelope shared by every Kind.

name.description: DNS-label resource name. Unique per namespace/kind within a manifest set.

namespace.description: DNS-label namespace. Defaults to default when omitted.

domain.description: DNS-label governance/segmentation domain (docs/adr/022). Defaults to "default" when omitted. Declaring domains and a policy.datascape.io crossDomain rule enables Ring 0 validate-time cross-domain denial and Ring 1 per-domain network segmentation; undeclared/all-default domains are a byte-identical no-op.

observers.description: Provider names whose connection details are resolved to LineageEndpoints and forwarded to this resource's provider if it is LineageAware.

protect.description: When true, plan/apply/destroy refuse any action that would delete this resource. Remove this field and re-apply to allow deletion.

accessGrant.description: docs/adr/026 §2 wide grant: under the GraphScopedAccess gate (docs/planning/08 H7), a resource reaches exactly the endpoints its declared references imply UNLESS it also declares spec.access entries — an explicit, reviewable, policy-visible opt-in to reach every OTHER resource in the named namespace, not just the ones it references. Absent (the default) is the minimal edge set; a wide grant is never implicit. A policy.datascape.io matchGrant rule can deny or otherwise govern a declared grant at validate time. docs/adr/033 decision 3 (docs/planning/08 K3): an optional selector narrows the audience to namespace AND selector — the bare namespace-wide form (no selector) keeps working but is deprecated and lint-flagged (DL022).

namespace.description: Every resource in this namespace (further narrowed by selector, if set) becomes reachable, in addition to the ordinary graph-derived edge set.

selector.description: docs/adr/033 decision 3 (docs/planning/08 K3): a Kubernetes-style label selector over the candidate resource's metadata.labels — when set, only namespace members whose labels satisfy it are admitted (audience = namespace AND selector). Evaluated only under the LabelScopedAccess feature gate (the SAME gate K2 registered); with the gate disabled a selector-bearing grant is INERT (admits nobody), never falling back to the wider bare namespace-wide form.

docs/adr/033 (Stage K2/K3): a Kubernetes-style label selector — matchLabels (equality, ANDed) plus matchExpressions (In/NotIn/Exists/DoesNotExist, ANDed with matchLabels and with each other). Mirrors schemas/policy/v1alpha1/policy.json#/$defs/selector exactly (internal/domain/policy.Selector is the single shared Go type both schemas describe; the two JSON Schema documents are independent embedded-schema graphs, so the shape is duplicated here rather than cross-file $ref'd — manifest resources and Policy documents compile through two separate jsonschema.Compiler instances, internal/application/manifest/schema.go and internal/application/policy/schema.go). At least one of matchLabels/matchExpressions must be set.

values.description: Required (non-empty) for In/NotIn; must be absent/empty for Exists/DoesNotExist.
